message:
chore(conformance): align core_* fixtures with real-redis semantics

Fixture refresh matching real-redis observed behaviour for MIGRATE
port deferral, unknown-subcommand error taxonomy, PFDEBUG sparse/
dense decode, and assorted small-frame shape updates. The fr-command
side of this work (parse_migrate_request deferral, pfdebug_cmd,
COMMAND GETKEYSANDFLAGS, debug/object unknown-subcommand error
strings) is held back due to an active file reservation by another
agent on crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs -- these fixtures will be
waiting when that commit lands.

crates/fr-command/tests/golden/migrate_*.golden:
 - migrate_basic / migrate_auth / migrate_auth2 / migrate_copy_replace:
   port field replaced by port_arg byte vector to reflect that the
   request now carries the raw argv bytes (parsed only at execute
   time).
 - migrate_invalid_port.golden used to record "Error: InvalidInteger"
   (eager parse); now records the full successfully-parsed
   MigrateRequest because port validation is deferred.

Fixtures:
 - core_migrate: MIGRATE with bogus/negative/decimal/empty port on
   a key that does not exist now expects "+NOKEY" simple string,
   matching real redis.
 - core_errors / core_debug / core_object: unknown-subcommand
   strings aligned to the CONFIG/OBJECT "unknown subcommand 'X'"
   form vs the DEBUG/LATENCY/SLOWLOG "unknown subcommand or wrong
   number of arguments for 'X'" form.
 - core_pfdebug: new decode/encoding expectations for sparse/dense
   HLL representation including the promotion boundary.
 - core_acl, core_client, core_config, core_connection,
   core_function, core_generic, core_strings: minor shape
   updates matching the refreshed command dispatcher responses.
